body: LHV Bank UK is a fast-growing digital bank building retail and business banking products for the UK market. I lead engineering for the Retail Banking division: 18 engineers across cross-functional squads spanning native mobile, backend engineering, and software engineering in test.

The role sits at the intersection of people leadership, technical strategy, and organisational scale. I work closely with Lead and Principal Engineers to set technical direction and raise the bar on engineering quality, while partnering with the wider Engineering SLT on initiatives to establish high-performance management practices across the function.
highlights: Managing and developing 18 engineers across three disciplines, building the individual growth pathways, feedback loops, and team health practices that let people do their best work inside a regulated environment.
Partnering with Lead and Principal Engineers to shape technical standards, reduce delivery risk, and ensure architecture decisions hold up as the product scales.
Leading hiring across multiple disciplines and building the talent pipeline needed to deliver scalable, customer-focused banking products at pace.
Contributing to Engineering SLT initiatives on high-performance management, helping establish consistent standards for how engineering teams are led across the organisation.

body: ClearScore gives millions of people free access to their credit score and a marketplace to find cards, loans, and mortgages that fit. I led the Growth Delta squad, a cross-functional team focused on user engagement and revenue experimentation across the UK Cards and Loans products.
highlights: Launched the Credit Scanner initiative, delivering a 4× lift in click-through from CRM campaigns and establishing a repeatable activation framework the team has used ever since.

Spotted data integrity issues in the Open Banking stack affecting nearly a third of marketplace records. Took ownership, drove the fix, and got it down to ~4%. The impact on downstream product performance was pretty meaningful.

Started a cross-squad collaboration group across the 6-squad UK GTM mission, mostly because I kept noticing teams solving the same problems in isolation. It became a genuine knowledge-sharing forum with real traction.
Led the technical transition to Kafka and Server Driven UI across the UK Marketplace, partnering with two squads and staying accountable for business metrics throughout the rollout.

main.role: Team Lead & Manager → Senior iOS Engineer · Auckland
body: Timely makes scheduling and point-of-sale software for the beauty industry. I joined as their sole iOS engineer, became the technical lead for mobile, then moved into management and eventually led mobile strategy across two organisations after the Evercommerce acquisition in 2021.

In the management chapter, I grew and ran the mobile team, took on interim management of the 8-engineer Payments group during an org transition, and defined architecture and development standards across Timely and SalonBiz.
highlights: Delivered the first Tap to Pay on iPhone integration in Australasia, a technically complex initiative that needed close coordination with Apple, Stripe, and internal platform teams.
Drove a multi-team Stripe terminal integration and a custom inventory management system tailored to beauty salons, balancing UX, scalability, and reliability.
Mentored engineers from junior through to senior promotion and led hiring and onboarding of diverse talent, including interns who went on to full-time roles.
As the original sole iOS engineer, built the Consult app from scratch: a standalone form builder with a custom DSL to generate native UI, PDF export, and full backend sync.

earlier.description: Built Circle's foundational iOS app from MVP, architecting key technical domains including a custom HTML renderer for rich native content. Acted as hiring manager for the iOS team and handed over a production codebase that supported Circle's growth to a $200M valuation.
Sole developer for both iOS and Android at a personal finance platform. Implemented a shared MVVM architecture across Swift and Kotlin, then onboarded and upskilled the next mobile hire.
Built an Android companion app for a BLE-enabled smart hot-desking system, deployed across Xero campuses. Integrated real-time hardware communication via a custom sockets layer.

Early career at a video-tech startup founded by Chad Hurley (YouTube co-founder). Shipped the collaborative video editing Android app and led iOS development for a pivoted product built on WebRTC. The video capture tech became core to the eventual Bluejeans acquisition.
